meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no design skills (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master User Onboarding Without Design Skills: Student Guide
content.seo_description: Stop letting design gaps stall your startup. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code as a student developer.
content.answer_block: You don't need a designer to build world-class onboarding. By leveraging AI-first tools like Antigravity for UI generation and Claude Code for logic implementation, you can create functional, professional onboarding flows that guide users to their 'Aha!' moment, turning your lack of design skills into a streamlined, high-conversion technical advantage.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding out the backend, only to stare at a blank, intimidating screen. The functionality is there, but the interface feels like a ghost town. The trap most student developers fall into is thinking they need a degree in graphic design to make their app feel 'real.' You don't. You need a system that translates your technical logic into an intuitive user journey.

In this guide, we strip away the myth that design is about aesthetics. Design is about communication. We will use Antigravity to scaffold your UI and Claude Code to weave the logic, transforming your app from a collection of endpoints into a polished, professional product that users actually understand how to navigate.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Before touching code, identify the single action that proves your app's value. We build the onboarding path to lead directly to this interaction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a user onboarding flow schema for a SaaS app that focuses on reaching the 'Aha!' moment within three steps, emphasizing simplicity and clear call-to-actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze my current project structure and suggest the most critical path to the user's first successful action.'

steps.title: Scaffold UI with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, functional UI shells. Focus on readability and standard spacing rather than custom graphics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive, minimalist onboarding modal component using Tailwind CSS that includes a progress bar, a descriptive title, and a single primary action button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the generated Antigravity modal into the main landing page route.'

steps.title: Implement State-Driven Logic
steps.description: Onboarding is just a state machine. Use Claude Code to ensure the user only sees the onboarding screens if they are new.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook that tracks user onboarding progress and persists state in local storage to prevent repeat displays.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a useOnboarding hook that checks for a finishedOnboarding flag and manages the visibility state of the modal.'

steps.title: Add Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Instead of full tutorials, use tooltips to explain features exactly when the user reaches them in the workflow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a lightweight, accessible tooltip component that triggers on hover and provides context-aware help for specific input fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Integrate the tooltip component into the core dashboard inputs to provide feature discovery.'

steps.title: Automate Success Feedback
steps.description: When a user finishes onboarding, reward them. Use subtle animations to confirm their progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide CSS animation code for a 'success' state that triggers when a user completes the final step of the onboarding process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a success callback to the onboarding flow that triggers a confetti animation or a celebratory toast message.'

pro_tips: Never design from scratch; use 'Design Systems' like Radix or Shadcn to force consistency without needing an eye for color theory.
Limit your onboarding to 3 steps maximum. If it takes longer, your product is too complex, not your design.
Use 'Empty States' as onboarding opportunities. If there is no data, tell the user exactly what to do to get it.
Focus on copy over visuals; a clear, bold headline is more effective than a high-end illustration.
Track onboarding drop-off points using simple console logs before worrying about enterprise-grade analytics tools.
content.next_steps: Now that your onboarding is functional, deploy your app to Vercel and share the link with three real users to watch where they get stuck. Use those insights to iterate on your flow.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio is waiting for a project that actually converts users. Don't let a lack of design skills stop you from shipping your vision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
